items.headline: City Council approves Seeds of Peace Monument artwork at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: San José City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by artist Mario Chiodo to be located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The project represents a permanent public art installation at one of the city's major park spaces.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District budget approved for 2026-27
items.summary: City Council took action on the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District's 2026-2027 budget and annual assessment. The BID, which covers a commercial corridor in East San José, sets its operational funding and property owner assessment fees for the coming fiscal year.

items.headline: Health and Safety Element of General Plan Updated
items.summary: Cupertino City Council reviewed updates to the Health and Safety Element of the General Plan covering 2015–2040. This element guides how the city addresses public health, emergency preparedness, and safety infrastructure as the community grows.

items.headline: City Council appoints permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution to appoint a permanent City Manager. This fills a leadership role responsible for day-to-day city operations and implementing council policy.

items.headline: Duplex permitted on Old Piedmont Road in San José
items.summary: A new two-story, two-unit duplex has been permitted at 2174 Old Piedmont Road, representing a modest increase in housing density in the area. At just over $1 million, this is the only multi-unit residential project in this permit batch.

items.headline: City unveils draft Urban Water Master Plan and shortage contingency strategy
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will review Sunnyvale's Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. This addresses how the city manages water supply and plans for drought conditions affecting the South Bay.

items.headline: Spring cycle opens for community events and neighborhood grants
items.summary: The City Council will consider 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program applications in the spring cycle on May 18. Residents and organizations can learn which community projects and events will receive city funding.

items.headline: Council to hear residential electrification status and proposed programs
items.summary: Palo Alto is scheduled to hear an update on residential electrification progress and consider proposed principles for new electrification assistance programs. These programs will help residents comply with air quality regulations.
